behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names ChatInterface, fill_height, AppConfig, AppTree, reload(), and root column scale; file paths like init.svelte.ts, types.ts, Blocks.svelte are derivable from the described scope.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what's broken (height hardcoded, fill_height not propagated) without giving specific line changes or code.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_init_svelte_scale_from_fill_height requires the scale assignment to be in init.svelte.ts specifically, and test_blocks_svelte_forwards_fill_height requires fill_height to appear in >=2 config contexts in Blocks.svelte; an alternative valid fix that propagates config differently (e.g., via a different file or single context) would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Python tests instantiate ChatInterface and check chatbot.height, config dict; subprocess runs prettier/ruff. Frontend tests are text-based but justified since TS/Svelte cannot execute from Python.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests check actual values like ci.chatbot.height equals Chatbot default and config['fill_height'] is True/False, requiring real implementation.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All four expected behaviors (no hardcoded height, scale from fill_height, AppConfig type, config in AppTree+reload) are tested.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped by section headers (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Pass-to-pass, Config-derived) with docstrings.

no_typos.reason: No typos detected in paths or names.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction describes scope ('backend ChatInterface component and frontend initialization code') without naming specific files or lines.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: Code-only task, not an agentmd-edits config task.
